seed_expert_discoveries.technical_takeaway: New routing system using GLM 5.2 + Kimi 2.7 (open-source) for prompts, benchmarked against xHigh (Opus 4.8 + GPT 5.5) for cost/performance tradeoffs in a general agent/super assistant.
Emerging trend of composing AI systems from multiple specialized subagents rather than monolithic agents, signaling a shift in agent architecture design.

global_discoveries.technical_takeaway: Open-source framework for multi-agent orchestration with shared tasks, agent-to-agent messaging, reviews, logs, and human oversight.
Survey of memory paradigms (lightweight semantic, entity-centric, episodic/reflective, hierarchical graphs+vectors) with agentic control via RL for store/retrieve/update in long-term agent recall.
Redis paper on semantic caching to reduce LLM costs/latency via reuse of similar queries; emphasizes evaluation metrics like P-CHR AUC over raw benchmarks.

near_misses.technical_takeaway: Shift from human task lists to structured backlogs with explicit scope, repo context, and acceptance criteria for safe agent delegation.
near_misses.reason: Insightful but more conceptual than a specific new tool/repo/breakthrough.

notes: Very limited high-signal technical posts in the 24h window from seeds or global searches matching strict criteria. Focused on agent orchestration, memory, and efficiency patterns. No major new repos or benchmarks beyond the curated items.
